DefinitionDescription: A modified prothrombin time is performed using diluted thromboplastin reagent which lowers the phospholipid concentration, which optimizes sensitivity to Lupus Anticoagulants (LA). Standard prothrombin tests are usually normal in patients with lupus anticoagulants because of the high concentration of phospholipids in normal thromboplastin reagent. Lupus anticoagulants are antibodies which inhibit the coagulation results of phospholipid-dependent tests including APPT, KCT,dRVVT, and dPT. The dPT test screens for the phospholipid-dependent inhibitors of the extrinsic pathway of the coagulation cascade. Information from PA Labs, LLC-Midwest Coag (personal communication) and www.labcorp.com, accessed 2008 02 28.
